text: Ecology
the study of how organisms interact with each other and their environments
original_text: Ecology is the study of how organisms interact with each other and their environments.

text: connects living things with climate, soil, water, sunlight, and available resources
original_text: Ecology connects living things with climate, soil, water, sunlight, and available resources.

text: an ecosystem includes living organisms and the nonliving factors around them
original_text: An ecosystem includes living organisms and the nonliving factors around them.

text: A habitat is the place where an organism lives
original_text: A habitat is the place where an organism lives.

text: A niche describes the role an organism plays in its ecosystem
original_text: A niche describes the role an organism plays in its ecosystem.

text: populations are groups of the same species living in the same area
original_text: Populations are groups of the same species living in the same area.

text: communities include all the different populations living together in an area
original_text: Communities include all the different populations living together in an area.

text: biomes are large regions with similar climates, plants, and animals
original_text: Biomes are large regions with similar climates, plants, and animals.

text: energy enters most ecosystems through sunlight
original_text: Energy enters most ecosystems through sunlight.

text: producers make their own food using sunlight or chemical energy
original_text: Producers make their own food using sunlight or chemical energy.

text: plants, algae, and some bacteria are producers
original_text: Plants, algae, and some bacteria are producers.

text: consumers get energy by eating other organisms
original_text: Consumers get energy by eating other organisms.

text: herbivores eat plants or other producers
original_text: Herbivores eat plants or other producers.

text: carnivores eat other animals
original_text: Carnivores eat other animals.

text: omnivores eat both plants and animals
original_text: Omnivores eat both plants and animals.

text: decomposers break down dead organisms and recycle nutrients
original_text: Decomposers break down dead organisms and recycle nutrients.

text: fungi and bacteria are important decomposers
original_text: Fungi and bacteria are important decomposers.

text: food chains show one pathway of energy flow
original_text: Food chains show one pathway of energy flow.

text: food webs show many connected feeding relationships
original_text: Food webs show many connected feeding relationships.

text: energy decreases as it moves up trophic levels
original_text: Energy decreases as it moves up trophic levels.

text: only a fraction of energy transfers from one trophic level to the next
original_text: Only a fraction of energy transfers from one trophic level to the next.

text: nutrients cycle through ecosystems instead of flowing one way
original_text: Nutrients cycle through ecosystems instead of flowing one way.

text: the carbon cycle moves carbon through air, organisms, water, and rocks
original_text: The carbon cycle moves carbon through air, organisms, water, and rocks.

text: the nitrogen cycle makes nitrogen available to living things
original_text: The nitrogen cycle makes nitrogen available to living things.

text: the water cycle moves water through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and runoff
original_text: The water cycle moves water through evaporation, condensation, precipitation, and runoff.

text: competition occurs when organisms need the same limited resource
original_text: Competition occurs when organisms need the same limited resource.

text: predation occurs when one organism hunts and eats another
original_text: Predation occurs when one organism hunts and eats another.

text: symbiosis describes close relationships between different species
original_text: Symbiosis describes close relationships between different species.

text: mutualism benefits both species involved
original_text: Mutualism benefits both species involved.

text: commensalism benefits one species while the other is mostly unaffected
original_text: Commensalism benefits one species while the other is mostly unaffected.

text: parasitism benefits one organism while harming another
original_text: Parasitism benefits one organism while harming another.

text: predator and prey populations can influence each other over time
original_text: Predator and prey populations can influence each other over time.

text: carrying capacity is the maximum population an environment can support
original_text: Carrying capacity is the maximum population an environment can support.

text: population growth depends on births, deaths, immigration, and emigration
original_text: Population growth depends on births, deaths, immigration, and emigration.

text: limiting factors restrict population size
original_text: Limiting factors restrict population size.

text: food, water, shelter, disease, and predators can all be limiting factors
original_text: Food, water, shelter, disease, and predators can all be limiting factors.

text: density-dependent factors become stronger as population density increases
original_text: Density-dependent factors become stronger as population density increases.

text: density-independent factors affect populations regardless of density
original_text: Density-independent factors affect populations regardless of density.

text: natural disasters can strongly affect ecosystems
original_text: Natural disasters can strongly affect ecosystems.

text: disturbances can damage ecosystems but also create opportunities for new growth
original_text: Disturbances can damage ecosystems but also create opportunities for new growth.

text: ecological succession is the gradual change of a community over time
original_text: Ecological succession is the gradual change of a community over time.

text: primary succession begins where there is no existing soil
original_text: Primary succession begins where there is no existing soil.

text: secondary succession begins after a disturbance where soil remains
original_text: Secondary succession begins after a disturbance where soil remains.

text: pioneer species are early organisms that help prepare an area for later life
original_text: Pioneer species are early organisms that help prepare an area for later life.

text: biodiversity is the variety of life in an ecosystem
original_text: Biodiversity is the variety of life in an ecosystem.

text: high biodiversity can make ecosystems more stable and resilient
original_text: High biodiversity can make ecosystems more stable and resilient.

text: low biodiversity can make ecosystems more vulnerable to change
original_text: Low biodiversity can make ecosystems more vulnerable to change.

text: keystone species have unusually large effects on their ecosystems
original_text: Keystone species have unusually large effects on their ecosystems.

text: removing a keystone species can cause major ecological changes
original_text: Removing a keystone species can cause major ecological changes.

text: invasive species can disrupt ecosystems when they spread outside their native ranges
original_text: Invasive species can disrupt ecosystems when they spread outside their native ranges.

text: invasive species may outcompete native species for resources
original_text: Invasive species may outcompete native species for resources.

text: native species evolved in a particular region over time
original_text: Native species evolved in a particular region over time.

text: endangered species are at risk of extinction
original_text: Endangered species are at risk of extinction.

text: extinction means a species no longer exists
original_text: Extinction means a species no longer exists.

text: habitat loss is a major cause of species decline
original_text: Habitat loss is a major cause of species decline.

text: pollution can harm organisms, water, soil, and air
original_text: Pollution can harm organisms, water, soil, and air.

text: climate change affects ecosystems by shifting temperatures, precipitation, and habitats
original_text: Climate change affects ecosystems by shifting temperatures, precipitation, and habitats.

text: ocean acidification affects marine ecosystems by changing seawater chemistry
original_text: Ocean acidification affects marine ecosystems by changing seawater chemistry.

text: deforestation removes habitats and changes carbon storage
original_text: Deforestation removes habitats and changes carbon storage.

text: overfishing can disrupt marine food webs
original_text: Overfishing can disrupt marine food webs.

text: conservation protects species, habitats, and ecological processes
original_text: Conservation protects species, habitats, and ecological processes.

text: restoration ecology tries to repair damaged ecosystems
original_text: Restoration ecology tries to repair damaged ecosystems.

text: protected areas can help preserve biodiversity
original_text: Protected areas can help preserve biodiversity.

text: wildlife corridors help animals move between habitats
original_text: Wildlife corridors help animals move between habitats.

text: sustainable resource use aims to meet human needs without destroying ecosystems
original_text: Sustainable resource use aims to meet human needs without destroying ecosystems.

text: ecologists study patterns at many scales, from microbes to entire biomes
original_text: Ecologists study patterns at many scales, from microbes to entire biomes.

text: field observations help ecologists understand real ecosystems
original_text: Field observations help ecologists understand real ecosystems.

text: experiments help test ecological ideas
original_text: Experiments help test ecological ideas.

text: mathematical models help predict ecological change
original_text: Mathematical models help predict ecological change.

text: remote sensing can track forests, oceans, ice, and land use from above
original_text: Remote sensing can track forests, oceans, ice, and land use from above.

text: ecology often studies relationships rather than isolated organisms
original_text: Ecology often studies relationships rather than isolated organisms.

text: A small change in one species can affect many others
original_text: A small change in one species can affect many others.

text: ecosystems are networks, not simple piles of organisms
original_text: Ecosystems are networks, not simple piles of organisms.

text: ecological balance does not mean nothing changes
original_text: Ecological balance does not mean nothing changes.

text: healthy ecosystems can still experience disturbance, competition, and death
original_text: Healthy ecosystems can still experience disturbance, competition, and death.

text: energy flow and nutrient cycling are central ideas in ecology
original_text: Energy flow and nutrient cycling are central ideas in ecology.

text: organisms shape their environments while being shaped by them
original_text: Organisms shape their environments while being shaped by them.

text: helps explain why species live where they do
original_text: Ecology helps explain why species live where they do.

text: helps explain why ecosystems respond differently to stress
original_text: Ecology helps explain why ecosystems respond differently to stress.

text: helps explain how life stays connected across landscapes
original_text: Ecology helps explain how life stays connected across landscapes.

text: forests store carbon, support biodiversity, and influence climate
original_text: Forests store carbon, support biodiversity, and influence climate.

text: grasslands support grazing animals and deep-rooted plants
original_text: Grasslands support grazing animals and deep-rooted plants.

text: wetlands filter water, store carbon, and provide habitat
original_text: Wetlands filter water, store carbon, and provide habitat.

text: coral reefs support enormous biodiversity in shallow tropical oceans
original_text: Coral reefs support enormous biodiversity in shallow tropical oceans.

text: desert ecosystems are adapted to limited water
original_text: Desert ecosystems are adapted to limited water.

text: tundra ecosystems are shaped by cold temperatures and short growing seasons
original_text: Tundra ecosystems are shaped by cold temperatures and short growing seasons.

text: freshwater ecosystems include rivers, lakes, ponds, and wetlands
original_text: Freshwater ecosystems include rivers, lakes, ponds, and wetlands.

text: marine ecosystems include oceans, reefs, estuaries, and deep-sea environments
original_text: Marine ecosystems include oceans, reefs, estuaries, and deep-sea environments.

text: urban ecology studies how living things survive in human-built environments
original_text: Urban ecology studies how living things survive in human-built environments.

text: humans are part of ecosystems, not separate from them
original_text: Humans are part of ecosystems, not separate from them.

text: reveals that survival is often about connection
original_text: Ecology reveals that survival is often about connection.

text: no organism exists completely alone in nature
original_text: No organism exists completely alone in nature.

text: ecosystems can look peaceful while intense competition happens underneath
original_text: Ecosystems can look peaceful while intense competition happens underneath.

text: messy because living systems are messy
original_text: Ecology is messy because living systems are messy.

text: turns nature into a map of relationships
original_text: Ecology turns nature into a map of relationships.

text: ecology the science of everyone being in everyone else's business
original_text: The meatball considers ecology the science of everyone being in everyone else's business.

text: nature's group chat, except every message affects survival
original_text: Ecology is nature's group chat, except every message affects survival.

text: shows that the sauce of life is shared, recycled, and occasionally chaotic
original_text: Ecology shows that the sauce of life is shared, recycled, and occasionally chaotic.

text: makes a forest look less like scenery and more like a living argument
original_text: Ecology makes a forest look less like scenery and more like a living argument.

text: asks how living things connect, compete, cooperate, and survive together
original_text: At its core, ecology asks how living things connect, compete, cooperate, and survive together.
